title: PARTNER DASHBOARD
subtitle: Manage codes, track earnings, grow your network
pageDescription: Manage your partner codes and track earnings

application.metaDescription: Create the partner workspace foundation, complete the staged application, and submit the package into the governed backend review flow.
application.title: Partner Workspace Application
application.subtitle: This is the first real onboarding loop for the separate partner realm: create the workspace baseline, complete the staged application draft, and submit it into the governed backend review flow.

summary.notSaved: No local draft has been saved yet.

stages.title: Application stages
stages.description: The portal keeps workspace, profile, compliance, and review as separate steps so the governed activation model stays explicit.

workspace.label: Workspace
workspace.description: Establish the partner workspace shell and declare the primary lane.

profile.label: Profile
profile.description: Capture the starter organization profile that partner ops will review first.

compliance.label: Compliance
compliance.description: Record the baseline traffic and disclosure attestations.

review.label: Review
review.description: Check readiness and mark the draft as prepared for backend submission.

workspace.intro: Create the minimum workspace identity for the portal. This stage stays intentionally small so applicants can start quickly without collapsing the later review flow.

profile.intro: Add the starter organization profile and channel context. The goal is not full KYB yet, but enough signal for fit review and routing.

compliance.intro: Keep traffic and disclosure expectations explicit. These attestations stay separate from general terms because they drive review and governance later.
compliance.notice: This baseline declaration does not replace later finance, tax, or lane-specific compliance work. It captures the first mandatory partner-side attestations.

items.disclosure: I will clearly disclose the material connection when promoting CyberVPN.
items.fakeReviews: I will not use fake reviews, misleading testimonials, or deceptive privacy claims.
items.brandBidding: I will not brand-bid or launch undeclared traffic methods without written approval.
items.audit: I accept evidence requests, traffic review, and compliance audits when required.

review.intro: Review the current draft, verify the staged inputs are complete, and submit the package into the backend review queue.
review.validation: Complete the workspace, profile, and compliance stages before marking the application ready.
review.backendNote: Draft data is now persisted in the backend. Local storage remains only as an autosave cache so in-progress edits are not lost before the next sync.

fields.complianceAccepted: I confirm the disclosure, traffic, and compliance baseline for this application draft.

placeholders.businessDescription: Describe the business model, audience, and why CyberVPN fits.
placeholders.acquisitionChannels: SEO content, Telegram channels, paid media, communities, owned storefronts...

creatorAffiliate.label: Creator / Affiliate
creatorAffiliate.description: Owned content, communities, comparison pages, or creator-led promotion.

performanceMedia.label: Performance / Media
performanceMedia.description: Declared paid traffic, postback-ready campaigns, and tighter launch controls.

resellerApi.label: Reseller / API
resellerApi.description: Distribution, storefront, or API resale with legal and support responsibilities.

saveFeedback.saved: Draft synced with the partner backend.
saveFeedback.error: We couldn't sync the application draft with the partner backend.

submitFeedback.withdrawn: Application moved back to draft state.
submitFeedback.workspaceRequired: A backend workspace draft is required before the application can continue.
submitFeedback.error: We couldn't update the backend application flow.

application.readOnlyMode: This role can review the application foundation, but cannot edit or submit it.

linkedWorkspaces.title: Next portal surfaces
linkedWorkspaces.description: Use the canonical route map now: `/organization` will become the durable business profile surface, while `/settings` holds security and session controls.

organization.metaDescription: Manage the canonical partner organization profile, operating regions, and key contacts for the active workspace.
organization.title: Organization Profile
organization.subtitle: This surface stores the canonical organization profile for the active workspace and keeps partner identity, contacts, and operating context aligned with backend truth.

summary.notSaved: No backend save has been recorded yet for this workspace profile.
summary.reviewReady: Application draft is currently marked ready for review.
summary.reviewPending: Application draft still needs more organization details before review.

placeholders.primaryLane: Choose a lane in the application surface first
placeholders.businessDescription: Describe the partner organization, audience, and market fit.
placeholders.acquisitionChannels: SEO content, Telegram channels, newsletters, communities, paid media...

saveFeedback.error: We couldn't save the workspace organization profile.

organization.readOnlyMode: This role can review the organization profile, but cannot edit it.

readiness.title: Why this matters
readiness.description: The organization profile is now a backend-owned workspace surface used by review, routing, finance, and support flows.

items.workspace: Keep the active workspace identity aligned with its business-facing profile.
items.contacts: Maintain named contacts for support, technical, and finance operations.
items.geo: Keep regions and languages current for lane review and routing.
items.channels: Preserve business and channel context for review, compliance, and analytics surfaces.

settings.metaDescription: Manage workspace settings, delivery preferences, and operator-facing security posture for the active partner workspace.
settings.title: Workspace Settings
settings.subtitle: This surface is now backed by canonical workspace settings: identity summary, notification preferences, and workspace security posture all persist through backend contracts.

summary.notSaved: No backend save has been recorded yet for these workspace settings.
summary.cookieModel: Auth uses cookie-backed partner sessions; deeper MFA and active-session controls remain follow-on backend milestones.

identity.title: Account identity
identity.description: Use the authenticated partner session and workspace role as the current identity read model.

preferences.title: Preferences
preferences.description: Notification and delivery preferences are now saved through the workspace settings contract.

security.title: Security posture
security.description: These controls persist as workspace security posture, even where enforcement remains tied to later auth milestones.

workspaceSecurityAlerts.label: Security alerts
workspaceSecurityAlerts.description: Receive alerts when partner access or governance posture changes.

payoutStatusEmails.label: Payout status emails
payoutStatusEmails.description: Receive future finance and settlement state notifications in the workspace inbox and email.

productAnnouncements.label: Program announcements
productAnnouncements.description: Receive portal release notes, enablement updates, and program communications.

requireMfaForWorkspace.label: Plan MFA enforcement
requireMfaForWorkspace.description: Record that the workspace intends to require MFA when the backend enforcement hooks are available.

preferPasskeys.label: Prefer passkeys
preferPasskeys.description: Mark passkeys as the preferred future sign-in method for this workspace.

reviewedActiveSessions.label: Review active sessions
reviewedActiveSessions.description: Confirm the workspace has reviewed current session hygiene before launch.

saveFeedback.error: We couldn't save the workspace settings.

nextSteps.title: What comes later
nextSteps.description: The workspace settings contract is live, but some security and delivery capabilities still depend on later backend rollout.

items.mfa: Real MFA setup, enforcement, and audit hooks.
items.notifications: Server-backed delivery preferences and inbox routing.

tasks.empty: No pending tasks for the current scenario.

business_profile.title: Expand business profile
business_profile.description: Partner ops needs more detail about the workspace profile, website, and audience fit.

owned_channels.title: Prove owned channels
owned_channels.description: Add working owned properties, social links, or creator surfaces that can be reviewed.

traffic_methods.title: Clarify traffic methods
traffic_methods.description: Explain traffic sources, acquisition methods, and any paid or declared distribution plans.

support_ownership.title: Clarify support ownership
support_ownership.description: Show who owns partner-side support and storefront operations for the workspace.

finance_profile.title: Complete finance profile
finance_profile.description: Finance onboarding still needs payout-profile and settlement-contact information.

finance.title: Blocked pending finance
finance.description: The workspace may remain visible, but payout actions and finance expansion stay blocked until payout onboarding is complete.

compliance.title: Blocked pending compliance
compliance.description: Evidence or declarations are still missing, so compliance-driven actions remain task-bound instead of becoming fully operational.

technical.title: Blocked pending technical readiness
technical.description: Integrations and launch tooling stay limited until technical setup and validation are finished.

application_review.title: Application review thread
application_review.description: Core review thread between the workspace and partner ops.

requested_info.title: Requested info loop
requested_info.description: Ops requested more information before the application can continue.

finance_onboarding.title: Finance onboarding
finance_onboarding.description: Settlement profile, payout contact, or finance baseline still needs partner action.

compliance_followup.title: Compliance follow-up
compliance_followup.description: Compliance and governance signals remain visible during probation and review.

restriction_notice.title: Restriction notice
restriction_notice.description: A governance or finance issue is reducing what the workspace can do right now.

attribution_dispute.title: Attribution dispute
attribution_dispute.description: This case tracks attribution explainability questions, ownership disputes, and follow-up with partner ops.

payout_dispute.title: Payout dispute
payout_dispute.description: This case keeps payout holds, release questions, and settlement disagreements inside the governed portal loop.

technical_support.title: Technical support
technical_support.description: Use this case for postback, storefront, integration, or implementation issues that need partner-side action.

statement_question.title: Statement question
statement_question.description: This case explains reserves, adjustments, or line-item settlement questions without exposing internal payout tools.

workspace_draft.title: Workspace draft created
workspace_draft.description: The workspace exists, but the application has not entered governed review yet.

application_submitted.title: Application staged for review
application_submitted.description: The staged application is ready and the partner-facing review loop can begin.

application_needs_info.title: Application needs information
application_needs_info.description: Partner ops requested more profile, traffic, or compliance context before review can continue.

application_approved_probation.title: Probation approval issued
application_approved_probation.description: The workspace may enter a limited governed launch path while remaining under review controls.

application_waitlisted.title: Application waitlisted
application_waitlisted.description: Activation is deferred for fit, timing, or capacity reasons, but the workspace is not declined.

application_rejected.title: Application rejected
application_rejected.description: The current application closed and the workspace remains outside the active partner surface.

needs_info.title: More information requested
needs_info.description: Partner ops asked for additional profile, traffic, or compliance context.

review_in_progress.title: Review in progress
review_in_progress.description: The application is back with partner ops and is currently being reviewed.

waitlisted.title: Application waitlisted
waitlisted.description: The workspace is not declined, but activation is deferred for fit, timing, or capacity reasons.

probation_started.title: Probation started
probation_started.description: The workspace can enter a limited launch path while remaining governed.

lane_membership_changed.title: Lane membership changed
lane_membership_changed.description: A lane approval or decline updated the governed commercial surface for this workspace.

review_request_opened.title: Review request opened
review_request_opened.description: A requested-information task is open and now needs a partner response.

case_created.title: Case opened
case_created.description: A governed support or operational case is active for this workspace.

case_reply_received.title: Case reply received
case_reply_received.description: A new reviewer or operations reply arrived in an active partner case.

legal_acceptance_required.title: Legal acceptance required
legal_acceptance_required.description: A current legal document version must be accepted before rollout can continue.

statement_ready.title: Statement ready
statement_ready.description: A settlement statement closed and is now available in finance.

payout_blocked.title: Payout blocked
payout_blocked.description: Finance or governance posture is blocking payout execution until readiness is restored.

integration_delivery_failed.title: Integration delivery failed
integration_delivery_failed.description: A reporting or postback delivery path failed and needs partner-side follow-up.

governance_action_applied.title: Governance action applied
governance_action_applied.description: Workspace restrictions changed and the partner-facing surface is now constrained.

finance_blocked.title: Finance actions blocked
finance_blocked.description: Payout actions remain blocked until finance readiness is restored.

payout_profile_needed.title: Payout profile needed
payout_profile_needed.description: Finance onboarding is required before the workspace can fully settle earnings.

workspace_restricted.title: Workspace restricted
workspace_restricted.description: Governance and finance signals reduced the current operational surface.

workspace_active.title: Workspace active
workspace_active.description: The governed activation path completed and the workspace can operate with full active-surface visibility.

sectionAccess.blockedDescription: {section} is not available for the current workspace state. Keep the applicant-safe loop in application, cases, and inbox until the status advances.
sectionAccess.releaseRingBlockedDescription: {section} is held back by the current portal release ring. The module opens after rollout advances from {currentRing} to {requiredRing}.
sectionAccess.roleBlockedDescription: {section} is not available for the current workspace role {role}. Switch to a role with access or continue in dashboard and settings.
sectionAccess.banner: This section is currently {access} because the workspace is {status}.

cases.metaDescription: Review requested information, application cases, and the applicant-safe message loop for the partner workspace.
cases.title: Support & Cases
cases.subtitle: Applicants and early-stage partners can now stay inside the portal while answering partner ops, review, finance, and compliance requests.

restrictions.title: Restricted-operating posture
restrictions.description: Cases remain visible during restricted or governed states so the workspace can remediate finance, compliance, and launch issues without using internal ops tools.
restrictions.fallbackNote: This restriction remains backend-governed for the current workspace.

requestedInfo.title: Requested information
requestedInfo.description: Open review requests stay visible here instead of being hidden in settings or external email threads.
requestedInfo.responsePlaceholder: Summarize what was uploaded, clarified, or handed off for partner ops.
requestedInfo.success: Response added to the partner ops thread.
requestedInfo.error: Could not send the review response.
requestedInfo.threadEmpty: No partner responses have been recorded for this review request yet.
requestedInfo.empty: No open requested-info tasks are active for the current scenario.

modes.applicant: The workspace is still in applicant-safe mode: requested information, application review, and early support questions stay grouped in one governed surface.
modes.operational: The workspace can use the broader support surface for live operational questions, attribution follow-up, and statement clarifications.
modes.dispute: The workspace is operating in a constrained dispute posture: remediation, payout disputes, and governance-sensitive cases remain visible while expansion is blocked.
modes.history_only: The workspace can inspect historical case records, but the live operational support loop is no longer active.

guidance.title: How to use this surface
guidance.description: PP2 keeps the applicant communication loop inside the portal even before the full backend case platform is live.

items.application: Update the staged application if partner ops asked for more context.
items.organization: Use the organization surface to fill business profile, regions, and named contacts.
items.compliance: Expect compliance and traffic questions to remain explicit rather than buried in terms.
items.notifications: Keep the inbox open for status changes and reminders.

caseList.title: Case timeline
caseList.description: Each case stays attached to the workspace status instead of disappearing when the portal is not fully active yet.
caseList.threadEmpty: No partner workflow messages have been recorded for this case yet.
caseList.responsePlaceholder: Add the next partner-side update for ops, finance, or compliance review.
caseList.readySuccess: Case marked ready for partner ops.
caseList.responseError: Could not update the case workflow.

notifications.metaDescription: Review inbox events, workflow reminders, and blocked-state explanations for the partner workspace.
notifications.title: Notifications & Inbox
notifications.subtitle: The inbox is now a real partner surface: review changes, blocked-state reminders, finance signals, and probation notices are grouped in one feed.

feed.title: Inbox feed
feed.description: The inbox now reads canonical backend events for review, finance, compliance, and governed workspace changes.
feed.empty: No active notifications are currently visible for this workspace and role.

blockedStates.title: Blocked-state explanations
blockedStates.description: Readiness overlays remain separate from the canonical status. Use these explanations to understand why a module is limited even when the workspace still exists.

team.metaDescription: Review workspace members, launch roles, deferred roles, and role-aware access posture for the partner workspace.
team.title: Team & Access
team.subtitle: PP3 introduces the external workspace RBAC layer: launch roles, deferred roles, member posture, and role-aware access for the operating portal.

members.title: Workspace members
members.description: The team surface now reads canonical membership records, role assignments, and limited member posture from the backend workspace domain.
members.empty: No workspace members are visible for the current workspace yet.

launchSet.title: Launch role set
launchSet.description: These are the must-have external roles for the first delivery slice.

deferredRoles.title: Deferred but modeled roles
deferredRoles.description: These roles stay modeled from day one even if some capabilities ship later.

actions.title: Administration posture
actions.manageAllowed: This role can manage workspace team membership or delegated administration inside the launch surface.
actions.manageBlocked: This role can inspect team posture, but delegated administration stays with owner-level or admin roles.

editor.lookupPlaceholder: Enter partner operator email or login
editor.error: Could not update workspace team membership.

programs.metaDescription: Review canonical lane memberships, readiness posture, manager context, and rollout blockers for the partner workspace.
programs.title: Programs
programs.subtitle: Programs is the canonical lane-operations surface. It separates lane posture, rollout readiness, and owner context from generic workspace settings.

lanes.title: Lane memberships
lanes.description: Lane posture stays separate from workspace status so review, probation, activation, and restrictions remain explainable from backend-owned contracts.
lanes.readinessEmpty: No extra readiness notes are attached to this lane yet.
lanes.empty: No canonical lane memberships are available for the current workspace posture.

readiness.title: Workspace readiness
readiness.description: Finance, compliance, and technical posture stay backend-owned so pilot and lane decisions do not collapse into local UI heuristics.

actions.title: Program actions
actions.writeAllowed: This role can review backend-owned lane posture and use linked workspace surfaces to progress finance, compliance, and support tasks.
actions.readOnly: This role can inspect backend-owned lane posture, but write actions remain constrained to delegated owner, admin, or traffic workflows.

items.status: Track per-lane status without collapsing it into one workspace flag.
items.restrictions: Keep blockers, probation, and rollout warnings visible beside each lane.
items.manager: Show owner and manager context before deeper workflow actions are taken.

laneApplications.title: Lane applications
laneApplications.description: Request additional lane access through the canonical backend review flow instead of relying on local or email-only handoffs.
laneApplications.readOnly: This role can inspect lane applications, but new requests remain limited to owner, admin, or delegated operator roles.
laneApplications.empty: No lane applications have been recorded yet for this workspace.
laneApplications.placeholder: Describe the business case, launch intent, traffic posture, and why this lane should be opened for the workspace.
laneApplications.submitError: The lane application could not be submitted.
laneApplications.workspaceRequired: Select a workspace before requesting a lane.

legal.metaDescription: Review legal documents, policy acceptance state, and owner-level contract responsibilities for the partner workspace.
legal.title: Contracts & Legal
legal.subtitle: Contracts, payout terms, policy history, and owner-level responsibilities now live in one dedicated legal surface instead of leaking into generic settings.

documents.title: Legal document set
documents.description: These document cards now come from the canonical workspace legal contract and reflect acceptance state recorded on the backend.

ownerActions.title: Owner-level actions
ownerActions.allowed: This role can handle the narrow owner-level actions that stay sensitive even when the rest of the workspace is delegated.
ownerActions.blocked: This role can review documents and policy history, but owner-level legal actions remain restricted.

items.contracts: Final contract acceptance where policy requires owner authority.
items.payoutDestination: Settlement destination changes that alter payout ownership.

codes.metaDescription: Review partner codes, starter tracking inventory, and lane-aware code capabilities for the workspace.
codes.title: Codes & Tracking
codes.subtitle: PP4 opens the first real commercial operating loop: starter codes, vanity links, QR packs, and attribution-ready macros now appear with lane-aware launch limits.

inventory.title: Code inventory
inventory.description: The code list is now lane-aware. Creator gets the widest probation launch, Performance stays more controlled, and Reseller remains storefront-sensitive.
inventory.empty: No codes are available for the current workspace state and lane.

modes.starter: This lane can use a starter launch path now, but expansion tools stay gated behind early quality checks and readiness overlays.
modes.controlled: This lane can operate, but code scale-out stays deliberately constrained by approval, governance, or storefront alignment.
modes.full: This lane can use the full PP4 code surface for standard growth operations.
modes.read_only: The workspace can inspect existing code posture, but new launch actions stay blocked until the restriction clears.

actions.title: Action posture
actions.starter: This role can work with the starter launch inventory, but broader code scale-out remains gated.
actions.controlled: This role can manage the controlled launch set, but certain code types still require extra approval or lane readiness.
actions.full: This role can use the PP4 code surface as the current lane allows.
actions.readOnly: This role or workspace state can inspect code posture, but create or expansion actions remain blocked.

campaigns.metaDescription: Review the campaign library, enablement kits, and lane-aware creative approval posture for the workspace.
campaigns.title: Campaigns / Assets / Enablement
campaigns.subtitle: Assets, launch kits, and approval-sensitive campaigns now live in one dedicated partner surface instead of being implied by codes alone.

library.title: Campaign library
library.description: Campaign assets remain lane-aware. Performance requires the strictest review path, while Creator and Reseller use different enablement depth.
library.empty: No campaign assets are available for the current workspace state and lane.

modes.starter: The lane can use starter enablement assets, but campaign expansion remains measured and review-aware.
modes.controlled: Campaign access exists, but submissions, approval, and library depth remain constrained by lane controls.
modes.full: The lane can use the full PP4 campaigns surface for standard launch and enablement work.
modes.read_only: The workspace can review current assets and approval posture, but cannot advance campaign operations right now.

actions.title: Action posture
actions.starter: This role can use starter campaign kits and review notes, but broader campaign rollout stays gated.
actions.controlled: This role can work with controlled campaign assets while approval-sensitive paths remain explicit.
actions.full: This role can use the current campaign surface at full lane depth.
actions.readOnly: This role or workspace state can inspect assets, but new campaign actions remain blocked.

submission.title: Request creative approval
submission.description: Submit a new creative or campaign scope into the canonical backend approval loop and keep its status visible in this surface.
submission.readOnly: This role can inspect current campaign posture, but new creative approval requests remain blocked.
submission.referencePlaceholder: Creative reference, landing page, or asset ID
submission.detailsPlaceholder: Describe the asset, placement, messaging guardrails, and any disclosure-sensitive notes.
submission.success: Creative approval request submitted for workspace review.
submission.error: The creative approval request could not be submitted.
submission.workspaceRequired: Select a workspace before submitting a creative approval request.

compliance.metaDescription: Review lane-specific compliance tasks, traffic declarations, and governance-facing remediation posture for the workspace.
compliance.title: Traffic & Compliance
compliance.subtitle: PP4 separates declarations, evidence, creative approvals, and governance-facing remediation into a dedicated partner-facing compliance center.

restrictions.title: Partner-visible restrictions
restrictions.description: These restrictions come from the canonical bootstrap posture. They explain why compliance actions remain governed even when the workspace is still visible.
restrictions.fallbackNote: Restriction details remain governed by backend posture for this workspace.

tasks.title: Compliance tasks
tasks.description: The task set is lane-aware. Performance adds postback and creative controls, while Reseller exposes support ownership and storefront-sensitive obligations.
tasks.empty: No compliance tasks are currently visible for this workspace state.

declarations.title: Traffic declarations
declarations.description: Backend-owned visibility keeps declared traffic sources, postback posture, and creative review scope attached to the active workspace instead of local portal-only assumptions.
declarations.empty: No backend-owned traffic declarations are currently visible for this workspace and role.

modes.review: The workspace is still in a review-bound posture, so compliance remains centered on declarations, clarifications, and evidence loops.
modes.starter: The lane can operate with a lighter compliance starter set, but evidence and escalation paths remain visible.
modes.controlled: The lane is governed by a controlled compliance posture with explicit declarations, approval steps, and readiness gates.
modes.full: The lane can use the full PP4 compliance surface for its current operational state.
modes.read_only: The workspace can review tasks and remediation context, but the current route posture remains read-only.

actions.title: Action posture
actions.review: This role can review the compliance loop and respond to review-bound requests, but the launch surface remains governed.
actions.starter: This role can work through the lighter compliance starter set while escalation controls remain explicit.
actions.controlled: This role can manage active compliance tasks for the current lane, including approval-sensitive controls.
actions.full: This role can use the current compliance surface at full lane depth.
actions.readOnly: This role or workspace state can inspect remediation context, but direct compliance actions are currently limited.
actions.workspaceRequired: An active workspace is required before compliance actions can be submitted.
actions.errorFallback: The compliance action could not be submitted. Try again.

traffic.title: Submit traffic declaration
traffic.description: Declare approved sources or postback readiness directly from the portal on the canonical workspace object.
traffic.success: Traffic declaration submitted for workspace review.

creative.title: Request creative approval
creative.description: Route creative review through the workspace-safe approval flow without exposing internal moderation controls.
creative.success: Creative approval request submitted for workspace review.

conversions.metaTitle: Conversions / Orders / Customers - Ozoxy Partner Portal
conversions.metaDescription: Review attributed conversions, order visibility, dispute context, and row-scoped customer explainability for the partner workspace.
conversions.title: Conversions / Orders / Customers
conversions.subtitle: PP6 adds the operational evidence layer: attributed conversions, order context, refund and dispute visibility, and row-scoped customer explainability by lane.

records.title: Attributed records
records.description: Records stay partner-facing and row-scoped. The portal exposes explainability and dispute context without leaking internal moderation tools.

explainability.title: Explainability
explainability.description: Explainability for {order} is loaded directly from the canonical backend order-resolution pipeline.
explainability.empty: Select a conversion record to inspect explainability and commissionability posture.
explainability.loading: Loading explainability from the canonical backend contract...
explainability.error: The explainability snapshot could not be loaded.
explainability.noReasonCodes: No explicit reason codes were attached to this evaluation.

modes.lite: Probation opens a lighter conversions surface with attributed records and explainability, but broader order and customer workflows stay governed.
modes.full: The workspace can use the full conversions surface for order visibility, commissionability review, and row-scoped customer context.
modes.dispute: The workspace remains in a constrained operational state, so disputes, refunds, and explainability stay visible while expansion actions are blocked.

analytics.metaDescription: Review KPI reporting, export posture, and explainability scope for the partner workspace.
analytics.title: Analytics & Exports
analytics.subtitle: PP5 turns reporting into a first-class partner surface: KPI visibility, explainability, export posture, and lane-aware reporting depth now live in one place.

metrics.title: Performance metrics
metrics.description: Metrics stay explainable across probation, active, and constrained states without exposing internal moderation or fraud tooling.

exports.title: Exports
exports.requestErrorFallback: The export request could not be recorded.
exports.workspaceRequired: Select a workspace before requesting exports.
exports.noRequests: No export requests have been recorded yet.

modes.review: Analytics remains review-bound until the workspace reaches probation or active reporting depth.
modes.lite: Probation opens a lighter analytics surface with core KPIs and narrower export scope.
modes.full: The workspace can use the full analytics surface for standard reporting and export workflows.
modes.history_only: The workspace can inspect reporting history and explainability context, but expansion-oriented analytics actions remain constrained.

finance.metaDescription: Review statements, payout account posture, reserves, and settlement readiness for the partner workspace.
finance.title: Finance
finance.subtitle: PP5 gives the workspace a real finance surface: statement visibility, payout-account posture, reserves, and explainable blocked finance states.

statements.title: Statements
statements.description: Statement visibility remains partner-facing and explainable without exposing internal payout execution or reserve override controls.
statements.emptyState: No statement history is visible for this workspace yet.

accounts.title: Payout accounts
accounts.description: Payout destinations, verification posture, and default selection now come from the canonical finance domain.
accounts.emptyState: No payout account is configured yet. Finance onboarding stays blocked until at least one payout destination is submitted.

blocked.title: Finance blockers
blocked.description: These blockers come from the canonical bootstrap and admin-driven finance controls.

eligibility.title: Payout eligibility
eligibility.description: Eligibility is calculated from payout-account verification, workspace state, and reserve or risk blockers.
eligibility.error: Eligibility could not be loaded right now.
eligibility.emptyState: Select a payout account to inspect its eligibility posture.

accountForm.title: Add payout account
accountForm.description: Finance onboarding is partner-facing: submit a payout destination and let finance ops verify it through the admin maker-checker flow.

fields.makeDefault: Make this the default payout destination

accountForm.success: Payout account submitted. Finance readiness will refresh after verification and bootstrap recompute.
accountForm.error: Payout account submission failed. Check the destination details and workspace state.

history.title: Payout history
history.description: This partner-facing history summarizes payout instructions and executions without exposing internal admin override tooling.
history.error: Payout history could not be loaded right now.
history.emptyState: No payout instruction or execution history is visible for this workspace yet.

modes.review: Finance remains in a review-only posture until the workspace enters probation or active settlement flows.
modes.onboarding: Probation opens finance onboarding: payout profile setup, statement preview, and forecast posture become visible before execution.
modes.full: The workspace can use the full finance surface for statement review and payout-readiness visibility.
modes.blocked: Finance visibility remains available, but payout-bearing actions are blocked until readiness or governance issues are resolved.
modes.history_only: The workspace can inspect historical finance records, but active finance operations are no longer available.

integrations.metaDescription: Review partner-side tokens, postbacks, webhook delivery, and technical readiness posture for the workspace.
integrations.title: Integrations
integrations.subtitle: PP6 opens the technical operating surface: reporting tokens, postback credentials, webhook delivery logs, and constrained diagnostics by lane.

credentials.title: Integration credentials
credentials.description: Credential posture stays partner-facing and explainable without exposing secret material or internal override tooling.

modes.selected: Selected creator use cases can inspect limited integrations, but broader technical operations remain gated.
modes.technical: The workspace can use the technical partner surface for diagnostics, token posture, and integration delivery visibility.
modes.blocked: Integration history remains visible, but launch-sensitive technical controls are constrained by readiness or governance.

reseller.metaDescription: Review storefront-linked scope, domains, pricebook preview, support ownership, and reseller technical posture.
reseller.title: Reseller Console
reseller.subtitle: PP6 opens the reseller-only operating surface: storefronts, support ownership, pricebook preview, and customer scope without leaking admin-level controls.

storefronts.title: Assigned storefronts
storefronts.description: Storefront visibility stays bounded to the reseller scope defined by contracts, assigned domains, and support ownership.

modes.operational: The reseller workspace can use its storefront-linked operational console within the assigned row-level scope.
modes.constrained: The reseller console remains visible for remediation, but expansion-oriented storefront actions stay constrained.

workspaceSwitcher.label: Active workspace
workspaceSwitcher.description: Switch the canonical workspace context for this portal session without relying on hidden query fallbacks.
workspaceSwitcher.sessionNote: This selection stays scoped to the current partner host until you switch again.
